name: Olm
primary_sources: Glory of the Duchy of Carniola by Valvasor

visual_attributes.morphology: snakelike body with a cylindrical trunk and a short, laterally flattened tail
distinctive_features: external gills forming two branched tufts
three toes on forelimbs and two toes on hind limbs
visual_attributes.texture: smooth skin with very little pigment
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: 20–30 cm long, with some specimens up to 40 cm
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: segmented trunk with regularly spaced furrows, small limbs with reduced digits

domain_specific_visual.NEOTENY: retains larval characteristics such as external gills into adulthood
domain_specific_visual.PHOTOSENSITIVITY: skin sensitive to light due to melanopsin

functional_attributes.primary_function: adapted to life in complete darkness in caves
secondary_functions: detects low concentrations of organic compounds in water
uses mechano- and electroreceptors for orientation

domain_specific_functional.SENSORY_ADAPTATION: enhanced smell and hearing for orientation in dark habitats
domain_specific_functional.ELECTRORECEPTION: registers weak electric fields and possibly uses Earth's magnetic field for orientation

relationships.description: The olm is endemic to the waters flowing underground through the limestone bedrock of the karst of Central and Southeastern Europe.
The olm is related to the genus Necturus within the family Proteidae.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Soča River basin, southern Slovenia, southwestern Croatia, Bosnia and Herzegovina
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Believed to be offspring of cave dragons by locals
primary_sources: Glory of the Duchy of Carniola by Valvasor
